votes.reason: Both the Coq and Python implementations use a placeholder for split_by_space that always returns an empty list, and the logic for validating the notes and beats matches exactly.

Both the Coq and Python implementations of split_by_space are placeholders that return an empty list for any input. Consequently, both parse_music_spec implementations require the result list to be empty for any input string, maintaining consistency.

Both the Coq and Python implementations use the same placeholder logic for split_by_space (always returning an empty list) and implement the same mapping for notes to beats in the specification check.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs. Both handle empty string by requiring result=[]. For non-empty strings, both require result length equals notes length, and both check that each note-beat pair matches one of the three valid combinations (o->4, o|->2, .|->1). The split_by_space placeholder returns [] in both versions, so behavior is consistent. The Python loop checking is equivalent to the Coq forall condition over valid indices.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs. Both handle empty string by requiring result=[]. For non-empty strings, both require result length equals notes length, and both check that each note-beat pair matches one of the three valid combinations (o->4, o|->2, .|->1). The split_by_space placeholder returns [] in both, so behavior is consistent. The Python loop checking is equivalent to the Coq forall condition over valid indices.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs. For empty music_string, both return/require result=[]. For non-empty strings, both use the same split_by_space (which returns [] in both), check length equality, and verify each note-beat pair matches one of the three valid combinations. The Coq split_by_space is a placeholder returning [] for non-empty strings, matching Python's behavior. The logic for checking valid note-beat pairs is equivalent.

split_by_space always returns [], so parse_music_spec accepts only an empty result list for any input, matching the Coq Prop. count_beats maps recognized notes to 4/2/1 and others to None, corresponding to Some values versus None in Coq.

split_by_space returns [] in both; parse_music_spec then accepts exactly result == [] for any music_string; count_beats maps notes to 4/2/1 or None consistently. Thus concrete behavior matches on all inputs.

count_beats maps notes to 4/2/1 or None (option wrapper difference only), split_by_space always returns [], and parse_music_spec enforces result == [] for empty and non-empty inputs accordingly; elementwise checks match the Coq spec with the placeholder split.
